propositions: Publication is gated only by a v* tag push check that never verifies the tagged SHA is an ancestor of reviewed main, allowing an arbitrary pushed tag to publish to PyPI, MCP-registry, and GitHub Release.

Version values in pyproject.toml, server.json top-level, and server.json packages[].version are kept in lockstep only by a manual runbook, risking disagreeing versions across channels with no machine check.
Release should be initiated via workflow_dispatch from main with a version-lockstep check and ancestry assertion running before build, and no push:tags publish trigger.
Build and publish should go directly via PyPA Trusted Publishing OIDC with no intermediate tag relay or PAT, creating the v* tag and GitHub Release only after a successful publish.
A release-time preflight must query the pypi environment protection and fail closed when required reviewers are absent or the deployment-branch policy is not main-only.
Guard helpers (version-lockstep, ancestry, env-preflight parsing) must be unit-tested and demonstrated to fire in a real dispatch run without producing any publish side effect.
